subject: URISN Q3 quarterly report — draft review
snippet: Gerald — please review the draft Q3 report before I send to Meridian...
body:
Gerald,

Please review the attached draft Q3 quarterly report before I send it to Tom Brennan at Meridian. Highlights from this quarter look strong — the Baltimore East cluster analysis is particularly compelling. Let me know if anything needs adjustment in the summary section.

Eleanor

subject: Re: URISN Q3 quarterly report — draft review
snippet: Eleanor — reviewed. Two edits in the summary...
body:
Eleanor,

Reviewed. Two suggested edits:
1. The incidence rate CI in Table 3 should read 95% not 90% — Marcus flagged this.
2. The executive summary undersells the neighborhood-level heterogeneity. I'll send a revised paragraph.

Looping Marcus for the stats correction.

Gerald

subject: Manuscript revision request — JUHE-2026-0417
snippet: Dear Dr. Roman — Reviewer 2 has requested clarification on your exposure assessment methodology...
body:
Dear Dr. Roman,

Thank you for your revised submission (JUHE-2026-0417). Reviewer 2 has requested one additional clarification on your exposure assessment methodology — specifically whether the satellite-derived PM2.5 estimates were validated against ground stations in the Baltimore metro area. Please address this in a point-by-point response. Deadline for resubmission is October 30, 2026.

Best,
Priya Natarajan
Editor, Journal of Urban Health Equity

subject: Re: Manuscript revision request — JUHE-2026-0417
snippet: Dr. Natarajan — thank you. I will address Reviewer 2's comment...
body:
Dr. Natarajan,

Thank you for the update. I will address Reviewer 2's question on PM2.5 validation — we did cross-validate against three EPA ground stations; I'll add a supplementary table. I'll have the resubmission in well before October 30.

Best,
Gerald Roman, PhD

subject: Year 3 grant renewal — timeline and requirements
snippet: Hi Gerald — just a heads-up on what Meridian needs for the Year 3 renewal package...
body:
Hi Gerald,

Following up on our call last week. For the URISN Year 3 renewal (due November 9, 2026), Meridian will need: (1) updated specific aims, (2) revised budget with justification, (3) progress report covering Year 2 milestones, and (4) updated dissemination plan. I'll send a formal template by June 15.

Tom Brennan
Program Officer, Meridian Public Health Foundation

subject: Re: Year 3 grant renewal — timeline and requirements
snippet: Tom — understood. We'll plan to have a draft to you by late October...
body:
Tom,

Understood. We'll target a complete draft renewal package by October 20, giving us time to review before the November 9 deadline. Looking forward to the template.

Gerald

subject: November 14 workshop — logistics and roster
snippet: Hi Gerald — the November workshop is filling up nicely...
body:
Hi Gerald,

The November 14 fingerstyle workshop is filling up — we have 9 confirmed students so far. I'll send the final roster by November 1. Can you confirm you'll need the usual setup (music stands, a PA for demo, two DI boxes)? Also, parking will be available in the rear lot this time.

Linda Osei
Dragonfly Music Center

subject: Re: November 14 workshop — logistics and roster
snippet: Linda — yes to the usual setup...
body:
Linda,

Yes to the usual setup — music stands, PA, two DIs. Nine students is a great number. I'll have printed handouts ready. Rear lot parking is great, thank you for arranging.

Gerald

subject: CAC rehearsal — June 7 at the community center
snippet: Hey — confirming June 7 rehearsal at 10am...
body:
Hey,

Confirming June 7 rehearsal at 10am at the Remington Community Center — Marcus and Dana are in. I'm adding two new tunes: a McKee piece and an arrangement I've been working on. Bring the Takamine if you can. Should be a good one.

Chris

subject: Re: CAC rehearsal — June 7 at the community center
snippet: I'll be there. Takamine it is...
body:
I'll be there. Takamine it is — the action on Grandma Dorothy's guitar is still a bit off after the humidity swing, so I'll leave her at home this time. Looking forward to the McKee piece.

Gerald

subject: Dad's appointment June 10
snippet: Honey — can you take Dad to Greenleaf on the 10th?...
body:
Honey,

Can you take Dad to Greenleaf Medical Associates on the 10th? His quarterly diabetes check is at 2pm. I have a double shift that day and can't swap it. He says he's fine to drive but you know how he is.

Love,
Mom

subject: Re: Dad's appointment June 10
snippet: I've got it. I'll pick him up at 1:30...
body:
I've got it. I'll pick him up at 1:30 so we have buffer. Will make sure he has his metformin log for the doc. Rest up after your shift.

Gerald

subject: Meeting request — dissertation methods chapter
snippet: Dr. Roman — I've revised the methods chapter...
body:
Dr. Roman,

I've revised the methods chapter per your last round of comments and I'd love to go over it with you before I submit to my committee. Could we meet the week of June 9? I'm flexible on timing. I can also come to CIPH if that's easier.

Thank you,
Helen Okafor

subject: URISN — updated regression tables
snippet: Gerald — attaching revised Table 3 with the corrected CIs...
body:
Gerald,

Attaching revised Table 3 with the corrected 95% CIs we discussed. The direction of the neighborhood SES coefficient holds; the confidence interval just narrowed slightly. I also ran sensitivity analyses excluding the two sites with <30 observations — findings are robust. Let me know if you want me to run anything else before the report goes out.

Marcus

subject: Re: URISN — updated regression tables
snippet: Perfect — the narrowed CI actually makes the argument cleaner...
body:
Perfect — the narrowed CI actually makes the argument cleaner. The sensitivity analyses are exactly what Reviewer 2 will want to see. Please drop both tables in the shared GitHub repo under /tables/v4. I'll incorporate them into the manuscript draft this weekend.

Gerald

subject: FINALS WEEK — send snacks please
snippet: Gerald I am drowning in pharmacology...
body:
Gerald I am drowning in pharmacology. Can you FedEx me something from Whole Foods? Dark chocolate, those seaweed crackers, and maybe one of those good tea blends from Derek's. You know I can't eat dining hall food during finals. I love you and I will repay you in hugs.

Rachel

subject: Re: FINALS WEEK — send snacks please
snippet: Package going out tomorrow. Hang in there...
body:
Package going out tomorrow morning — FedEx 2-day. Dark chocolate (two bars), seaweed crackers, and the jasmine tea Derek just got in. Added a pair of his wool socks because Philadelphia is cold and I know you never pack enough layers. Pharmacology will not defeat you.

Gerald

subject: Site visit schedule — East Baltimore neighborhood partners
snippet: Dr. Roman — Yemi and I have confirmed three site visit slots for June...
body:
Dr. Roman,

Yemi and I have confirmed three site visit slots with the neighborhood partners for June 16, 18, and 24. All three sites have signed updated consent forms. Yemi will handle transportation logistics; I'll prepare the interview guide updates. Should we send the revised instruments to Dr. Hayes for review first?

Sarah Kim

subject: October gathering at my place — save the date
snippet: Yo — Saturday October 24, backyard gathering...
body: Yo — Saturday October 24, backyard gathering at my place starting around 5pm. Bring whoever you want. I'm doing a crab boil. Would love to have you play a few songs if you're up for it. Chris is in, Marcus Webb said maybe. Derek

subject: Abstract accepted — National Conference of Epidemiologists 2026
snippet: Dear Dr. Roman — your abstract has been accepted for an oral presentation...
body:
Dear Dr. Roman,

We are pleased to inform you that your abstract "Neighborhood-Level Respiratory Illness Clustering in Baltimore: A Multi-Year Surveillance Approach" has been accepted for an oral presentation at the National Conference of Epidemiologists, October 15–17, 2026 in Chicago, IL. Session scheduling details will follow by August 15.

Conference Program Committee

subject: Brookhaven Arts Festival — November 7 slot confirmed
snippet: Hey all — we got the 4pm slot at Brookhaven...
body:
Hey all,

We got the 4pm slot on November 7 at the Brookhaven Arts Festival in DC. Load in is at 2:30pm. 45-minute set. I'll handle the parking pass. Dana, can you confirm the set list by mid-October? Gerald — you want to close with the Tommy Emmanuel medley again?

Marcus Webb

subject: Re: Brookhaven Arts Festival — November 7 slot confirmed
snippet: 4pm works. Yes to the Tommy Emmanuel close...
body:
4pm works. Yes to the Tommy Emmanuel close — same medley as the January show, it landed well. I'll update the chart. Dana, maybe open with the Iron & Wine arrangement you've been working on?

Gerald

subject: New listing alert: 3 homes matching your Charles Village search
snippet: Three new listings in Charles Village and Remington...
body: Three new listings in Charles Village and Remington match your saved search. Prices range from $285,000–$410,000. View all on Zillow.

subject: SPAM: Guitar teaching seminar — limited spots!!!
snippet: Earn $200/hr teaching guitar online with our system!!!
body:
Hi there!

Earn $200/hr teaching guitar online with our proven system!!! Limited spots available. Click here to register NOW!!!

snippet: Hey — confirming June 7 rehearsal at 10am...
body:
Hey,

Confirming June 7 rehearsal at 10am at the Remington Community Center — Marcus and Dana are in. I'm adding two new tunes: a McKee piece and an arrangement I've been working on. Bring the Takamine if you can. Should be a good one.

Chris

subject: Re: CAC rehearsal — June 7 at the community center
snippet: I'll be there. Takamine it is...
body:
I'll be there. Takamine it is — the action on Grandma Dorothy's guitar is still a bit off after the humidity swing, so I'll leave her at home this time. Looking forward to the McKee piece.

Gerald

subject: Dad's appointment June 10
snippet: Honey — can you take Dad to Greenleaf on the 10th?...
body:
Honey,

Can you take Dad to Greenleaf Medical Associates on the 10th? His quarterly diabetes check is at 2pm. I have a double shift that day and can't swap it. He says he's fine to drive but you know how he is.

Love,
Mom

subject: Re: Dad's appointment June 10
snippet: I've got it. I'll pick him up at 1:30...
body:
I've got it. I'll pick him up at 1:30 so we have buffer. Will make sure he has his metformin log for the doc. Rest up after your shift.

Gerald

subject: Meeting request — dissertation methods chapter
snippet: Dr. Roman — I've revised the methods chapter...
body:
Dr. Roman,

I've revised the methods chapter per your last round of comments and I'd love to go over it with you before I submit to my committee. Could we meet the week of June 9? I'm flexible on timing. I can also come to CIPH if that's easier.

Thank you,
Helen Okafor

subject: URISN — updated regression tables
snippet: Gerald — attaching revised Table 3 with the corrected CIs...
body:
Gerald,

Attaching revised Table 3 with the corrected 95% CIs we discussed. The direction of the neighborhood SES coefficient holds; the confidence interval just narrowed slightly. I also ran sensitivity analyses excluding the two sites with <30 observations — findings are robust. Let me know if you want me to run anything else before the report goes out.

Marcus

subject: Re: URISN — updated regression tables
snippet: Perfect — the narrowed CI actually makes the argument cleaner...
body:
Perfect — the narrowed CI actually makes the argument cleaner. The sensitivity analyses are exactly what Reviewer 2 will want to see. Please drop both tables in the shared GitHub repo under /tables/v4. I'll incorporate them into the manuscript draft this weekend.

Gerald

subject: FINALS WEEK — send snacks please
snippet: Gerald I am drowning in pharmacology...
body:
Gerald I am drowning in pharmacology. Can you FedEx me something from Whole Foods? Dark chocolate, those seaweed crackers, and maybe one of those good tea blends from Derek's. You know I can't eat dining hall food during finals. I love you and I will repay you in hugs.

Rachel

subject: Re: FINALS WEEK — send snacks please
snippet: Package going out tomorrow. Hang in there...
body:
Package going out tomorrow morning — FedEx 2-day. Dark chocolate (two bars), seaweed crackers, and the jasmine tea Derek just got in. Added a pair of his wool socks because Philadelphia is cold and I know you never pack enough layers. Pharmacology will not defeat you.

Gerald

subject: Site visit schedule — East Baltimore neighborhood partners
snippet: Dr. Roman — Yemi and I have confirmed three site visit slots for June...
body:
Dr. Roman,

Yemi and I have confirmed three site visit slots with the neighborhood partners for June 16, 18, and 24. All three sites have signed updated consent forms. Yemi will handle transportation logistics; I'll prepare the interview guide updates. Should we send the revised instruments to Dr. Hayes for review first?

Sarah Kim

subject: October gathering at my place — save the date
snippet: Yo — Saturday October 24, backyard gathering...
body: Yo — Saturday October 24, backyard gathering at my place starting around 5pm. Bring whoever you want. I'm doing a crab boil. Would love to have you play a few songs if you're up for it. Chris is in, Marcus Webb said maybe. Derek

snippet: Dear Dr. Roman — your abstract has been accepted for an oral presentation...
body:
Dear Dr. Roman,

We are pleased to inform you that your abstract "Neighborhood-Level Respiratory Illness Clustering in Baltimore: A Multi-Year Surveillance Approach" has been accepted for an oral presentation at the National Conference of Epidemiologists, October 15–17, 2026 in Chicago, IL. Session scheduling details will follow by August 15.

Conference Program Committee

subject: Brookhaven Arts Festival — November 7 slot confirmed
snippet: Hey all — we got the 4pm slot at Brookhaven...
body:
Hey all,

We got the 4pm slot on November 7 at the Brookhaven Arts Festival in DC. Load in is at 2:30pm. 45-minute set. I'll handle the parking pass. Dana, can you confirm the set list by mid-October? Gerald — you want to close with the Tommy Emmanuel medley again?

Marcus Webb

subject: Re: Brookhaven Arts Festival — November 7 slot confirmed
snippet: 4pm works. Yes to the Tommy Emmanuel close...
body:
4pm works. Yes to the Tommy Emmanuel close — same medley as the January show, it landed well. I'll update the chart. Dana, maybe open with the Iron & Wine arrangement you've been working on?

Gerald

Hi there!

Earn $200/hr teaching guitar online with our proven system!!! Limited spots available. Click here to register NOW!!!

subject: URISN Q3 quarterly report — draft review
snippet: Gerald — please review the draft Q3 report before I send to Meridian...
body:
Gerald,

Please review the attached draft Q3 quarterly report before I send it to Tom Brennan at Meridian. Highlights from this quarter look strong — the Baltimore East cluster analysis is particularly compelling. Let me know if anything needs adjustment in the summary section.

Eleanor

subject: Re: URISN Q3 quarterly report — draft review
snippet: Eleanor — reviewed. Two edits in the summary...
body:
Eleanor,

Reviewed. Two suggested edits:
1. The incidence rate CI in Table 3 should read 95% not 90% — Marcus flagged this.
2. The executive summary undersells the neighborhood-level heterogeneity. I'll send a revised paragraph.

Looping Marcus for the stats correction.

Gerald

subject: Manuscript revision request — JUHE-2026-0417
snippet: Dear Dr. Roman — Reviewer 2 has requested clarification on your exposure assessment methodology...
body:
Dear Dr. Roman,

Thank you for your revised submission (JUHE-2026-0417). Reviewer 2 has requested one additional clarification on your exposure assessment methodology — specifically whether the satellite-derived PM2.5 estimates were validated against ground stations in the Baltimore metro area. Please address this in a point-by-point response. Deadline for resubmission is October 30, 2026.

Best,
Priya Natarajan
Editor, Journal of Urban Health Equity

subject: Re: Manuscript revision request — JUHE-2026-0417
snippet: Dr. Natarajan — thank you. I will address Reviewer 2's comment...
body:
Dr. Natarajan,

Thank you for the update. I will address Reviewer 2's question on PM2.5 validation — we did cross-validate against three EPA ground stations; I'll add a supplementary table. I'll have the resubmission in well before October 30.

Best,
Gerald Roman, PhD

subject: Year 3 grant renewal — timeline and requirements
snippet: Hi Gerald — just a heads-up on what Meridian needs for the Year 3 renewal package...
body:
Hi Gerald,

Following up on our call last week. For the URISN Year 3 renewal (due November 9, 2026), Meridian will need: (1) updated specific aims, (2) revised budget with justification, (3) progress report covering Year 2 milestones, and (4) updated dissemination plan. I'll send a formal template by June 15.

Tom Brennan
Program Officer, Meridian Public Health Foundation

subject: Re: Year 3 grant renewal — timeline and requirements
snippet: Tom — understood. We'll plan to have a draft to you by late October...
body:
Tom,

Understood. We'll target a complete draft renewal package by October 20, giving us time to review before the November 9 deadline. Looking forward to the template.

Gerald

The November 14 fingerstyle workshop is filling up — we have 9 confirmed students so far. I'll send the final roster by November 1. Can you confirm you'll need the usual setup (music stands, a PA for demo, two DI boxes)? Also, parking will be available in the rear lot this time.

Linda Osei
Dragonfly Music Center

Yemi and I have confirmed three site visit slots with the neighborhood partners for June 16, 18, and 24. All three sites have signed updated consent forms. Yemi will handle transportation logistics; I'll prepare the interview guide updates. Should we send the revised instruments to Dr. Hayes for review first?

Sarah Kim

subject: Lease Renewal Offer - 3516 N Charles St Unit 3C
snippet: Hi Ms. Roman, please find attached the renewal offer for 3516 N Charles St Unit 3C. The new monthly rent will be $1,545 for the 12-month term.
body: Hi Ms. Roman, please find attached the renewal offer for 3516 N Charles St Unit 3C. The new monthly rent will be $1,545 for the 12-month term starting January 15, 2027, up from the current $1,485. Please sign via DocuSign at your earliest convenience. Best, Parkside Property Management.
